description: Kenya traffic class designations by cumulative MESA ranges for flexible pavement design. Kenya uses MESA (Million Equivalent Standard Axles) and CESA (Cumulative Equivalent Standard Axles) terminology -- never plain ESA.

terminology.MESA: Million Equivalent Standard Axles -- primary unit in RDM 3.4
terminology.CESA: Cumulative Equivalent Standard Axles -- total traffic over design life
terminology.MCESA: Million CESA -- equivalent to MESA for design purposes

notes: Kenya extends to TLC10 (80 MESA) compared to Uganda T8 (30M ESA)
MESA boundaries for TLC01-TLC08 numerically match Uganda T1-T8 ranges but use different terminology
TLC09-TLC10 cover traffic levels beyond Uganda MoWT catalogue range
For design traffic < 0.1 MESA, use low-volume road structures (RDM 3.4 Chapter 5: LV1-LV11)
If design MESA is near a class boundary, review forecasts and run sensitivity analysis
